article_title: JWST's COSMOS-Web Survey Maps 164,000 Galaxies Into the Most Detailed Cosmic Web Yet, Stretching Back to When the Universe Was a Billion Years Old
summary: All five direct quotes verified verbatim across five reachable snapshots; phys.org 403 and news.ucr.edu allowlist gap are not reader-facing.

content_preview.title: JWST's COSMOS-Web Survey Maps 164,000 Galaxies Into the Most Detailed Cosmic Web Yet, Stretching Back to When the Universe Was a Billion Years Old
content_preview.summary: A UC Riverside-led team used the largest JWST observing program to trace galaxies along the cosmic web from today out to redshift z ~ 7, and released the full pipeline and catalog publicly.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

An international team led by the University of California, Riverside has used the largest James Webb Space Telescope observing program to date to produce what its authors describe as the most detailed map of the cosmic web ever assembled, threading 164,000 galaxies through a contiguous patch of sky roughly the size of three full Moons. The work, published in The Astrophysical Journal, traces the network of galaxies, gas, and dark matter from the present day back to when the universe...

recommendations: Add news.ucr.edu to config/source_allowlist.txt — UC Riverside's official news service is the canonical primary source for UCR research and is already cited elsewhere in the archive.
phys.org has been intermittently 403ing bot snapshots; consider falling back to archive.org for that domain in chief:review.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece, 759 words (mid-News range), with Overview / What We Know / What Was Released / What We Don't Know sections. Clear technical framing; appropriate level of detail.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all five reachable snapshots. source-0 (UCR press release) confirms every direct quote verbatim, all five: 'JWST has completely changed our view of the universe, and COSMOS-Web was designed from the start to give us the wide, deep view we need to see the cosmic web' (Hatamnia); 'for the first time we can study the evolution of galaxies in cluster and filamentary structures across cosmic time, all the way from when the universe was a billion years old up to the nearby universe' (Hatamnia, also in Engadget); 'The jump in depth and resolution is truly significant, and we can now see the cosmic web at a time when the universe was only a few hundred million years old, an era that was essentially out of reach before JWST' (Mobasher); 'The telescope detects many more faint galaxies in the same patch of sky, and the distances to those galaxies are measured far more precisely' (Hatamnia); 'The pipeline used to build the map, the catalog of 164,000 galaxies and their cosmic density, and a video showing the cosmic web evolving across billions of years, has been released to the public' (Mobasher). source-1 (ScienceDaily), source-3 (Engadget), source-4 (EurekAlert), source-5 (SciTechDaily) corroborate the same quotes and specifics. source-2 (phys.org) returned HTTP 403 (bot-blocked) and was not snapshotted, but the specific claim attributed to phys.org (DOI 10.3847/1538-4357/ae5bac) is independently confirmed in sources 1, 4, and 5.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics traced: 164,000 galaxies, three-full-Moons sky area, z ~ 7 maximum redshift, 13.7 billion years cosmic history, paper title 'Large-Scale Structure in COSMOS-Web', DOI 10.3847/1538-4357/ae5bac, lead author Hossein Hatamnia, advisor Bahram Mobasher, Horizon 2020 funding, ten-country collaboration.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, factual. The 'What We Don't Know' section is editorially honest about the limits of the press materials.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of COSMOS-Web cosmic-web mapping. The two earlier May 2026 JWST stories in the archive (slow-rotator galaxy, buckyballs around a dying star) are unrelated topics.
concerns: phys.org snapshot failed (403). The phys.org-attributed claims are independently corroborated in the other snapshots, so no factual gap, but the manifest entry is null.
recommendations: Add news.ucr.edu to the source allowlist.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean submission. Every direct quote verified verbatim against the UCR press release; every specific traces to a snapshot. APPROVE — the two warnings (news.ucr.edu allowlist gap, phys.org 403) concern infrastructure, not reader-facing accuracy, so no corrections record is needed.
